expectations.text: Uses 'obsidian orphans' to find disconnected notes
expectations.evidence: guide.md Part 2 uses 'obsidian orphans' as the primary command, repeated in Quick Reference table and Summary Workflow section.

expectations.text: Uses 'obsidian unresolved' to find broken links
expectations.evidence: guide.md Part 3 uses 'obsidian unresolved' as the primary command, also appears in Quick Reference table, Summary Workflow, and Part 6 verification.

expectations.text: Uses 'obsidian deadends' to find dead-end notes
expectations.evidence: guide.md Part 4 uses 'obsidian deadends' as the primary command, also in Quick Reference table and Summary Workflow.

expectations.text: Uses 'obsidian tag tag="#archived"' (or similar correct syntax) to find archived notes
expectations.evidence: guide.md Part 5 Step 1 uses 'obsidian tag tag="#archived"' exactly, also in Quick Reference table and Summary Workflow.

expectations.text: Uses 'obsidian move file=... to=...' syntax for moving files (not cp or mv)
expectations.evidence: guide.md Part 5 Step 2 uses 'obsidian move file="1-Projects/old-project.md" to="4-Archive/"' and similar. Quick Reference table shows 'obsidian move file="path.md" to="4-Archive/"'. No cp or mv shell commands used for file moves.

expectations.text: CLI parameter style is param=value (Obsidian CLI convention), not --param
expectations.evidence: All CLI parameters throughout guide.md use param=value style (e.g., format=json, format=paths, tag="#archived", file="...", to="...", sort=modified, limit=20, name="tags", value='["done"]'). No --param style flags appear anywhere in the guide.

suggestions: Consider adding an expectation for correct vault-scoped commands (vault= prefix) to validate multi-vault awareness.
timing.json does not include an executor_duration_seconds field — consider adding it to the harness for finer-grained timing analysis.
eval_feedback.overall: Perfect pass on all 6 expectations. The guide correctly uses all required Obsidian CLI commands with proper param=value syntax, uses obsidian move instead of shell mv/cp, and demonstrates correct tag query syntax. The output is thorough and well-structured.
